ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Na EWURADZE see Noa d…õ, …îwo nye wo fie nyinara nk…î h…õn no mu; osiand…õ …îwo na mehu wo d…õ etsen w…î m‚Äôenyim w…î ber yi mu.
translated: And the LORD said to Noah, you and all your household shall enter the ark; for I have seen that you are righteous before Me in this generation.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Na afei wofi asaase a …îahan n‚Äôanomu regye wo nua no b…îgyaa w…î wo nsamu no do w…îadom wo:
translated: Now from the land that opens its mouth to receive your brother's corpse, you have placed his blood on your cloth and shown mercy to him:

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Na Abram twaa mu w…î asaase no do k…îr beebi a w…îfr…õ h…î Sekem, k…îr More esudur no ho. Na aber no nna Keenanfo w…î asaase no do.
translated: And Abram passed through the land to the place of Shechem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanites were then in the land.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Na Abram tutuu n‚Äôasesee, na …îbae b…õtsenaa Mamre nsudur a …îw…î Hebron no ne nky…õn, na osii af…îrmbukyia w…î h…î maa EWURADZE.
translated: And Abram moved his tent, and went and dwelt by the terebinth trees of Mamre, which are in Hebron, and built an altar there to the LORD.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Na …îbaa d…õ, aber a …îky…õ…õr h…î no, Filistiafo hen Abimelek hw…õ…õ tokura mu, na ohun d…õ Isaak nye ne yer Rebeka rowewe.
translated: And it came to pass, when he had been there a long time, that Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out at a window, and saw, and, behold, Isaac was sporting with Rebekah his wife.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankop…în b…î…î nyimpa w…î nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankop…în no su do na …îb…î…î nyimpa; …îb…î…î h…în …îbanyin nye …îbaa.
translated: And God created man in his own image, In the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho d…õ …îy…õ…õ nyimpa w…î asaase do, na …îy…õ…õ no yaw w…î n‚Äôakoma mu.
translated: The Lord saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.‚Äù
text: Na meb…õma m‚Äôahy…õmu ada emi nye …îwo ntamu, na meb…õma ad…îr mbordo.
translated: And I will give my testimony that it is not your fault, and I will give thanks to God.

ref: ‚ÄúI'm really lucky,‚Äù Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Na Leah see d…õ, Siar aba, na …îfr…õ…õ ne dzin Gad a asekyer…õ nye, Yiedzi.
translated: And Leah saw that, Siar came, and she named him Gad, which means, Yiedzi.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, ‚ÄúThis is God's camp.‚Äù So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun h…în no …îsee d…õ, Nyankop…în no d…îm nye yi: na …îfr…õ…õ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God's host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Na Nyankop…în no b…îfoe see me w…î adaaso mu d…õ, Jakob: na mesee d…õ, Emi nye yi.
translated: And God spoke to me in a vision, Jacob: and I said, It is I.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: gyed…õ dza mberants…õ no edzi no nko, na Aner, Eskol nye Mamre a w…înye me k…îree no h…în ky…õp…õn.
translated: the field of Ephron, which is in Machpelah, which is before Mamre, the field and the cave which is in it, and all the trees that are in the field, which are within all its borders around it,

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Na …îbaa d…õ, Josef dur no nuanom no nky…õn no, w…îp…î…îr, asaasaaba atar f…õ…õf…õw a …îhy…õ no no
translated: When the girl reached Joseph's relatives' place, they put on her a beautiful, colorful dress.

ref: ‚ÄúThis punishment is too hard!‚Äù Cain said.
text: Na Kain see EWURADZE d…õ, Mo b…în ho akatua so ky…õn d…õ mubotum asoa.
translated: And Cain said unto the LORD, My punishment is greater than I can bear.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza …îw…î mu nyinara y…õ.
translated: And the sky, the earth, and everything in them are not.
